# Phase 16.1: Phone-friendly auth UX for dashboard access - Context

**Gathered:** 2026-04-27
**Updated:** 2026-04-29 — TOTP 2FA folded into phase scope (Areas E + F appended). Basic Auth path killed per E-01.
**Status:** Ready for replanning (existing 16.1-01-PLAN.md and 16.1-02-PLAN.md must be regenerated by `/gsd-plan-phase 16.1` to reflect Area E + F decisions)

<domain>
## Phase Boundary

Add browser-friendly authentication paths to the dashboard so the operator can use `https://signals.<owned-domain>.com/` from a real iPhone (Safari + Chrome, no extension installed) without weakening the existing `X-Trading-Signals-Auth` header gate that curl/scripts and Phase 14 HTMX forms depend on.

After this phase, three valid auth paths exist:

1. **Header path (existing — Phase 13 D-01..D-06):** `X-Trading-Signals-Auth: <secret>` — used by `curl`, scripts, and Phase 14 HTMX trade-mutation forms. Unchanged.
2. **Basic Auth path (new — Plan 16.1-01):** `Authorization: Basic base64(username:secret)` — fallback for power-users who type `https://user:secret@signals…` in the URL bar; iOS Safari accepts this format and stores creds in Keychain. The system Basic-Auth dialog is **not** triggered server-side (no `WWW-Authenticate` header sent).
3. **Cookie path (new — Plan 16.1-02):** `GET /login` renders an HTML form; `POST /login` validates `username + secret`; success sets a 12-hour `itsdangerous`-signed `Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict` cookie. `POST /logout` clears the cookie and renders a confirmation page. Browser navigations without auth get **302 → /login** instead of `401 unauthorized`.

**Phase 16.1 requirements (9 — was 4 before 2026-04-29 TOTP fold-in):** AUTH-04 (iOS-native operator auth UX, no extension), AUTH-05 (new path additive — header path unchanged, HTMX forms keep working), AUTH-06 (auth strength preserved — env-only secret, `hmac.compare_digest`, single-operator scope), AUTH-07 (`/healthz` exempt; curl 401 contract preserved verbatim for non-browser requests), **AUTH-08 (TOTP enrollment via first-login web flow), AUTH-09 (TOTP verification on every cookie session start unless trusted-device cookie matches), AUTH-10 (per-device trusted-device cookie with revocation list), AUTH-11 (magic-link email reset to `mwiriadi@gmail.com` for lost-device recovery), AUTH-12 (Basic Auth path REMOVED — `Authorization: Basic` no longer accepted; cookie+TOTP is the only browser-friendly path).**

**Explicitly out of scope (deferred to future phases / v1.2):**
- Cloudflare Access / Zero Trust (Option 3 from todo) — would require re-orange-clouding DNS + rebuilding Let's Encrypt as DNS-01 (currently HTTP-01 since DNS is grey-clouded per UAT-16-A 2026-04-27). Architectural change; out of scope for an auth-UX phase.
- Magic link via Resend (Option 4 from todo) — requires the cookie-session machinery from Plan 16.1-02 PLUS email send-on-login. Heaviest UX. Captured in deferred for v1.2 if cookie+passwordless-flow becomes desired.
- Multi-user / OAuth / passkeys / WebAuthn — hard constraint per PROJECT.md "single-operator tool".
- Login-attempt rate limiting at nginx layer for `/login` — defense-in-depth follow-up; same rationale as Phase 13 D-Claude's-Discretion (rate-limit on `/` and `/api/state`). Captured in deferred; lift if `[Web] auth failure:` log noise grows.
- CSRF token machinery — `SameSite=Strict` cookie + single-form-per-site means CSRF surface is essentially nil. Skipped per Area D follow-up.

**Parallelizable with Phase 16?** Yes — Phase 16 (UAT completion) is operationally weekday-gated (UAT-16-B/C wait for a real drift email to land in Gmail). Phase 16.1 only needs Phase 13's `AuthMiddleware` to extend (loosened from "Depends on: Phase 16" to "Depends on: Phase 13" per ROADMAP.md insertion-commit note 2026-04-27).

</domain>

<decisions>
## Implementation Decisions

### Area A — Mechanism choice (LOCKED)

- **D-01: Phase 16.1 ships TWO new auth paths in one phase: Basic Auth (parallel to header) AND login form + signed cookie session.** Both layer onto Phase 13's `AuthMiddleware` (single ASGI chokepoint per Phase 13 D-01). Plan structure: 2 PLAN.md files. `16.1-01` = Basic Auth wave (~20-line middleware extension + env vars + setup-docs). `16.1-02` = login form + cookie wave (depends on 01; reuses the middleware helper that decodes Basic to also validate the cookie). Cookie middleware extension layers on Basic Auth's middleware diff. Both paths additive — header path (Phase 13 D-01..D-06) unchanged.

- **D-02: Cloudflare Access (todo Option 3) and magic link (todo Option 4) are deferred to v1.2.** Cloudflare Access requires re-orange-clouding (DNS is grey-clouded per UAT-16-A 2026-04-27 — `no Cloudflare proxy in path`) + rebuilding LE renewal as DNS-01 + creating Cloudflare API token + installing certbot-dns-cloudflare. Architectural change, not an auth-UX patch. Magic link is heaviest UX (must check email on first visit) and requires the cookie-session machinery underneath anyway. Captured in `<deferred>`.

- **D-03: AUTH-05 contract preserved verbatim.** All three paths must coexist — adding a new path NEVER removes or weakens the existing header path. `curl -H "X-Trading-Signals-Auth: <secret>" https://signals.<owned-domain>.com/` returns 200 with full dashboard HTML (test asserts byte-equality with pre-16.1 baseline if practical). HTMX forms in `dashboard.py` keep emitting `hx-headers='{"X-Trading-Signals-Auth": "{{WEB_AUTH_SECRET}}"}'` placeholder — D-13 below.

### Area B — 401 contract reconciliation (LOCKED, RECONCILES Phase 13 D-04)

- **D-04: Browser-conditional behavior is `302 → /login` redirect, NOT `WWW-Authenticate: Basic`.** A browser request (sniff: `Sec-Fetch-Mode: navigate` OR `Sec-Fetch-Dest: document` OR `Accept: text/html`) without valid auth returns `302 Location: /login?next=<original_path>`. The Basic Auth system dialog is never triggered server-side. Rationale: forms (D-09..D-14) deliver a polished UX (logo placeholder, friendly error messages, branded styling) that the browser system dialog cannot match; eliminating the dialog also avoids the `https://user:pw@host/` URL-bar ugliness.

- **D-05: Phase 13 D-04 letter relaxed only for browser navigation.** D-04's "no leaked info; no hints; plain-text body `unauthorized`" still holds for ALL non-browser requests:
  - `curl` without `Accept: text/html` and without `Sec-Fetch-Mode` headers → `401 unauthorized` plain-text, no `WWW-Authenticate` header, no redirect.
  - Scripts / API clients / HTMX XHR (XMLHttpRequest carries `Sec-Fetch-Mode: cors` or `same-origin`, NOT `navigate`) → `401 unauthorized` plain-text, no redirect.
  - Browser navigation (Sec-Fetch-Mode: navigate OR Accept: text/html via address-bar paste / link click) → `302 → /login`.

- **D-06: Basic Auth `Authorization: Basic ...` header is ACCEPTED if explicitly sent**, but the system dialog is never triggered. Operator can use `https://user:secret@signals.mwiriadi.me/` URL-bar form (Safari accepts, stores in Keychain). Curl `--user user:secret` works. The middleware decodes the Basic header, splits on first colon, validates `username == WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` AND `hmac.compare_digest(password.encode(), WEB_AUTH_SECRET.encode())`. On match → grant; on mismatch → fall through to whichever 302/401 path Sec-Fetch sniffing chose.

- **D-07: Sniff order in middleware** (after `EXEMPT_PATHS` per Phase 13 D-02):
  1. Decode `Authorization: Basic <b64>` if present → if valid → grant.
  2. Validate signed cookie (`tsi_session=<itsdangerous-token>`) if present → if valid AND not expired → grant.
  3. Validate `X-Trading-Signals-Auth` header if present → if `hmac.compare_digest` match → grant (Phase 13 D-03 unchanged).
  4. None matched → log failure (Phase 13 D-05 audit log unchanged) → branch on Sec-Fetch:
     - Browser navigation → `302 Location: /login?next=<original_path>` (D-04).
     - Anything else → `401 unauthorized` plain-text, no `WWW-Authenticate` (D-05).

### Area C — Credentials shape, env vars, validation (LOCKED)

- **D-08: TWO env vars required: `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` and `WEB_AUTH_SECRET`.** Both validated at `create_app()` boot per Phase 13 D-16/D-17 fail-closed pattern (RuntimeError → systemd Restart=on-failure surfaces in journald). `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` validation: non-empty (`len(username.strip()) > 0`), no length minimum, must NOT contain `:` (Basic Auth field separator — `:` in username breaks decode). `WEB_AUTH_SECRET` validation: existing Phase 13 D-17 ≥32 char check stays. Setup-docs (Phase 13 SETUP-DROPLET.md "Configure auth secret" section) gets a sibling step: pick a memorable username (`marc`, `operator`, etc.) — username has no security-entropy role here (password carries 128+ bits via D-17), so any printable non-`:` string is fine.

- **D-09: Realm string `"Trading Signals"` captured for completeness, but moot.** Since `WWW-Authenticate` is never sent (D-04..D-05), realm only appears if a future undo-decision changes course. If we ever start sending `WWW-Authenticate: Basic`, the realm value is `"Trading Signals"` (matches FastAPI app `title='Trading Signals'` in `web/app.py:88`).

### Area D — Login form + cookie session details (LOCKED)

- **D-10: Cookie crypto = `itsdangerous` `URLSafeTimedSerializer`.** New pinned dependency in `requirements.txt` (specific version pinned per CLAUDE.md §Stack convention — planner picks current latest stable, no `>=`). Mature single-purpose library (Pallets/Flask author), well-audited HMAC, built-in expiry checks via `loads(..., max_age=N)`, constant-time signature verification under the hood. ~5 lines to sign/verify in `web/middleware/auth.py`. Signing key is `WEB_AUTH_SECRET` (no new key env var — same secret already validated as ≥32 char per D-08).

- **D-11: Session TTL = 12 hours (`Max-Age=43200`).** Cookie is `Set-Cookie: tsi_session=<token>; Max-Age=43200; Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict; Path=/`. Operator logs in at start of trading day; session expires by next morning. iPhone Safari Keychain auto-fills cached creds on next-day re-login (one tap). The 12h TTL is also encoded inside the signed payload as `iat` (issued-at) timestamp, so `URLSafeTimedSerializer.loads(..., max_age=43200)` cross-checks the cookie-attribute Max-Age. Belt-and-suspenders against client-side cookie-attribute tampering.

- **D-12: `POST /logout` clears cookie + renders 'Logged out' confirmation page.** Endpoint accepts POST only (form-button on dashboard). Sets `Set-Cookie: tsi_session=; Max-Age=0; Path=/; Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict` (cookie-deletion attrs MUST match cookie-creation attrs per global LEARNINGS "Cookie deletion must match cookie creation options"). Renders an HTML page: title "Signed out", body "You're signed out. <a href='/login'>Sign in again</a>." with a footnote: "Basic Auth credentials may still be cached by your browser — close all Safari tabs to fully clear them." Honest about the iOS Keychain limitation. NO best-effort 401-eviction trick (browser behavior inconsistent across Safari/Chrome — not worth complexity).

- **D-13: HTMX forms in `dashboard.py` keep their existing `hx-headers='{"X-Trading-Signals-Auth": "{{WEB_AUTH_SECRET}}"}'` attribute UNCHANGED.** Zero diff to `dashboard.py` (1010..1099 form rendering, 1515..1565 partial-refresh trigger), zero diff to `web/routes/dashboard.py` placeholder substitution (Phase 14 Plan 14-04 Task 5 lines 73..76, 195..200) and its threat-T-14-15 mitigation tests in `tests/test_web_dashboard.py::TestAuthSecretPlaceholderSubstitution`. Belt-and-suspenders: HTMX form POST authenticates via the explicit header AND via the same-origin auto-sent cookie. If the cookie expires mid-day during a trade, the explicit header still authenticates the form — no 'sorry try again' confusion. AUTH-05 (additive contract) preserved verbatim. No CSRF token added (SameSite=Strict cookie + single-form-per-origin = no CSRF surface for a single-operator tool).

- **D-14: `GET /login` form structure** (planner's discretion on visual aesthetic, but contract-locked):
  - Two-field form: username (text) + secret (password)
  - Submit button: "Sign in"
  - On submit failure → re-render same form with single error message: "Sign in failed" (NO username-vs-password distinction per AUTH-02 spirit — "no leaked info; no hints"; same message + same response timing whether wrong username or wrong password)
  - On submit success → 302 to `?next=<path>` if `next` query param was set on incoming /login request (defaults to `/`)
  - Success path validates `next` is a relative path (starts with `/`, no `://`, no `.<scheme>:`) before redirecting — open-redirect prevention
  - Login form CSS: planner's discretion; mirror `dashboard.py::_INLINE_CSS` palette + typography for visual consistency. Shared CSS extraction is NOT required for this phase.

- **D-15: Folded Todo: `2026-04-27-phone-friendly-auth-ux-for-dashboard-access.md` (score 0.9).** Todo's "Problem" section becomes the source-of-truth narrative for AUTH-04 motivation. Todo's 4 candidate solutions: Options 1 + 2 fold into D-01 (this phase ships both); Options 3 + 4 fold into deferred (D-02). Todo's "Constraints to preserve" section maps directly to AUTH-05/AUTH-06/AUTH-07 (already in REQUIREMENTS.md per 2026-04-27 commit `cb33ff8`).

### Claude's Discretion

- **Plan-level wave structure inside 16.1-02.** Plan 16.1-02 (cookie wave) likely needs sub-tasks: itsdangerous serializer setup, GET /login handler, POST /login handler, POST /logout handler, AuthMiddleware cookie-validation extension, login form HTML template, /login + /logout tests. Planner sequences these.
- **Login form HTML template location.** Inline in `web/routes/login.py` (returned from handler as Jinja string) vs separate `web/templates/login.html` (introduces Jinja2 dependency if not already present — check `requirements.txt`). Planner picks; recommend inline if no other Jinja templates exist, or new `web/templates/` dir if Jinja2 is already pulled in.
- **`web/middleware/auth.py` refactor depth.** D-07's 4-step sniff order may justify extracting helpers (`_try_basic`, `_try_cookie`, `_try_header`, `_branch_unauthenticated`). Planner decides whether to refactor in 16.1-01, 16.1-02, or piecemeal.
- **Sec-Fetch header sniff fallback.** Modern browsers (Safari 14+, Chrome 76+, Firefox 90+) always send `Sec-Fetch-*` headers on navigations. Older browsers / non-browser HTTP clients don't. Planner: also fall back to `Accept: text/html` substring match for older/edge cases. If both signals miss, default to 401 plain-text (script-shaped).
- **Test file naming.** Recommend `tests/test_web_auth_basic.py` (Plan 16.1-01) and `tests/test_web_auth_cookie.py` + `tests/test_web_routes_login.py` (Plan 16.1-02), mirroring Phase 13's `tests/test_web_auth_middleware.py` naming.
- **`tests/test_web_auth_middleware.py` regression coverage.** Existing Phase 13 tests must keep passing unchanged (header path AUTH-05 preserved). Extend with new test classes — no rewrites of existing tests.
- **Whether to surface a "Sign out" button in `dashboard.py`.** UI nicety — small button in dashboard header / footer that POSTs to /logout. Planner discretion; recommend YES (otherwise the only logout path is typing `/logout` into the URL bar, which is awful UX). Renders only when a cookie session is detected (otherwise: no button, since header/Basic users can't meaningfully "log out" server-side).

### Folded Todos

- **`.planning/todos/pending/2026-04-27-phone-friendly-auth-ux-for-dashboard-access.md`** (score 0.9, area: auth) — operator pre-documented the problem, 4 candidate solutions, the lean (Basic Auth as v1.1.1 patch, cookie as proper v1.2 UX), and constraints. This phase ships both Option 1 + Option 2 in one go (per Area D answer). After 16.1 lands, this todo can be moved to `.planning/todos/completed/` by Plan 16.1-02's verification step.

### Area E — TOTP 2FA fold-in (LOCKED, 2026-04-29 — supersedes Areas A/D where indicated)

**Context:** Operator added 2FA requirement on 2026-04-29. Phase 16.1 originally shipped Basic Auth + cookie session as two paths. With 2FA mandatory, the URL-bar Basic Auth shortcut becomes incompatible (no place to type a 6-digit code in a URL) — so it's killed. Cookie login + TOTP becomes the sole browser-friendly path. Header path (`X-Trading-Signals-Auth`) is unchanged (used by curl/scripts/HTMX forms; no human at the keyboard, so 2FA does not apply).

- **E-01: Basic Auth path REMOVED. SUPERSEDES D-01, D-06, parts of D-07.** No `Authorization: Basic <b64>` decode in middleware. The `https://user:secret@signals.mwiriadi.me/` URL-bar form returns 302 → /login (browser navigation per D-04) just like a cold visit. iOS Keychain still autofills username + password into the /login form because Safari recognises the `signals.mwiriadi.me` origin from prior submissions — same UX, slightly more typing on first cold visit. Plan 16.1-01 is RESCOPED from "Basic Auth wave" to "Cookie login + TOTP enrollment + verify wave". `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` env var (D-08) is RETAINED (still used as the username field for cookie login).

- **E-02: D-07 sniff order is now 3 steps, not 4.**
  1. Validate signed cookie (`tsi_session=<itsdangerous-token>`) if present → if valid AND not expired AND TOTP-cleared per E-04 → grant.
  2. Validate `X-Trading-Signals-Auth` header if present → if `hmac.compare_digest` match → grant (Phase 13 D-03 unchanged).
  3. None matched → log failure (Phase 13 D-05 audit log unchanged) → branch on Sec-Fetch:
     - Browser navigation → `302 Location: /login?next=<original_path>` (D-04 unchanged).
     - Anything else → `401 unauthorized` plain-text, no `WWW-Authenticate` (D-05 unchanged).

- **E-03: TOTP enrollment via first-login web flow.** When operator submits valid username + password to `POST /login` AND no `totp_secret` exists in `auth.json` (E-09): server generates a fresh TOTP secret via `pyotp.random_base32()`, writes to `auth.json` with `enrolled=False`, sets a short-lived enrollment cookie (`tsi_enroll=<token>`, 10min), redirects to `GET /enroll-totp`. Enrollment page renders QR code (via `qrcode[pil]` library) of the otpauth URI `otpauth://totp/Trading%20Signals:<username>@signals.mwiriadi.me?secret=<base32>&issuer=Trading%20Signals` plus a manually-typeable secret string fallback. Form: 6-digit "verify" field. POST submit → server validates the code via `pyotp.TOTP(secret).verify(code, valid_window=1)` — if valid, set `enrolled=True` in auth.json AND issue full `tsi_session` cookie (per D-10..D-11) AND redirect to `/`. If invalid, re-render enrollment page with "Code didn't match — try again" (no leak of how many attempts).

- **E-04: TOTP verification on every cookie session start UNLESS trusted-device cookie matches (E-05).** When operator submits valid username + password to `POST /login` AND `auth.json.totp_secret` exists with `enrolled=True`: server checks for `tsi_trusted=<uuid>` cookie. If present AND uuid is in `auth.json.trusted_devices` AND `revoked=False`: skip TOTP, issue `tsi_session` cookie, 302 to `next`. Otherwise: set short-lived `tsi_pending=<token>` cookie (10min) with hashed payload `{username, password_verified=True, ip}`, redirect to `GET /verify-totp`. Verify page renders simple 6-digit input + "Trust this device for 30 days" checkbox (default UNCHECKED). POST submit → validate `pyotp.TOTP(secret).verify(code, valid_window=1)`. If valid: issue `tsi_session` (12h per D-11). If "trust this device" checked: ALSO issue `tsi_trusted` cookie (E-05). If invalid: re-render with same generic error.

- **E-05: Trusted-device cookie = `tsi_trusted=<itsdangerous-signed-uuid>`, 30-day Max-Age.** Cookie attrs: `Max-Age=2592000; Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict; Path=/`. Signed with `WEB_AUTH_SECRET` (same key as `tsi_session` per D-10). Server-side: each `tsi_trusted` issuance writes a row to `auth.json.trusted_devices`: `{uuid, label, granted_at, last_seen, revoked, revoked_at}`. Label format: auto-extract from User-Agent ("iPhone Safari", "macOS Chrome 122") + first 3 octets of IP at grant + grant date — e.g., `"iPhone Safari · 203.0.113.x · 2026-04-29"`. On every request that uses a `tsi_trusted` cookie successfully, update `last_seen` (atomic write, batched per request — every cookie hit updates last_seen, but disk write is fire-and-forget per Plan 16.1-N executor's discretion to avoid hot-path latency).

- **E-06: Per-device revocation via `/devices` page.** New route `GET /devices` (auth-required, cookie session only — header-path requests get 403 since header has no concept of "device"). Renders table: label, granted_at, last_seen, "Revoke" button per row. Current device marked `(this device)`. POST `/devices/revoke` with `{uuid}` flips `revoked=True` AND `revoked_at=now`. Revoked uuids are KEPT in auth.json (audit trail) but the middleware refuses any cookie whose uuid has `revoked=True`. UI polish: "Revoke all other devices" button (POST `/devices/revoke-all`) flips revoked=True on every uuid except the current one. Active session itself can revoke devices without re-prompting TOTP per E-08.

- **E-07: Email reset = magic link → log-in once + land on enrollment page → choose keep-or-replace.** `/login` page shows link "Lost 2FA? Reset via email" → POST `/forgot-2fa` with `{username, password}` (BOTH required — login-page link option per user). Server validates username + password (same `hmac.compare_digest` as cookie login) → if valid: generate magic-link token via `URLSafeTimedSerializer(secret).dumps({purpose: 'totp-reset', iat: now})`, write `{token_hash: sha256(token), email: 'mwiriadi@gmail.com', action: 'totp-reset', created_at: now, expires_at: now+3600, consumed: False}` to `auth.json.pending_magic_links`, send email via Resend with link `https://signals.mwiriadi.me/reset-totp?token=<token>` (1h TTL per E-08). Server response is ALWAYS the same generic page: `"If the credentials match an account, a reset link has been sent to the registered email"` — no leak of which credential was wrong, no leak of whether email was sent. (E-12 rate-limits to prevent enumeration via timing.) Click link → `GET /reset-totp?token=<token>` validates token (signature, expiry, not consumed) → marks consumed=True → issues `tsi_session` cookie (one-time bypass) → redirects to `GET /enroll-totp?reset=1`. Enrollment page in reset mode shows TWO buttons: "Keep current authenticator" (just redirect to /) AND "Set up new authenticator" (regenerate `totp_secret`, render new QR, require verify of new code before completing). Same destination either way — operator regains access.

- **E-08: Trusted active session = full trust for sensitive actions.** Add new trusted device, revoke devices, view recovery (N/A per E-07), reset TOTP via /enroll-totp manually — all available without re-prompting TOTP. Rationale: 12h cookie session is short; if laptop stolen mid-session, 12h is the worst-case exposure. Re-prompting TOTP for every sensitive action adds friction the operator explicitly opted out of.

### Area F — TOTP storage, email transport, magic-link mechanics (LOCKED, 2026-04-29)

- **F-01: New file `auth.json` adjacent to `state.json`.** Atomic writes mirror `state_manager.py::save_state` (tempfile + fsync + os.replace per CLAUDE.md §Architecture). Schema:
  ```json
  {
    "schema_version": 1,
    "totp_secret": "<base32>" or null,
    "totp_enrolled": false,
    "totp_enrolled_at": "<iso8601>" or null,
    "trusted_devices": [
      {"uuid": "<uuid4>", "label": "iPhone Safari · 203.0.113.x · 2026-04-29",
       "granted_at": "<iso8601>", "last_seen": "<iso8601>",
       "revoked": false, "revoked_at": null}
    ],
    "pending_magic_links": [
      {"token_hash": "<sha256_hex>", "email": "mwiriadi@gmail.com",
       "action": "totp-reset", "created_at": "<iso8601>",
       "expires_at": "<iso8601>", "consumed": false, "consumed_at": null}
    ]
  }
  ```
  `auth.json` is gitignored (mirror `.gitignore` entry for `state.json`). NEW module `auth_store.py` (NOT inside `web/` — pure I/O adapter, hex-lite peer of `state_manager.py`) exposes `load_auth()`, `save_auth(...)`, plus narrow helpers `get_totp_secret()`, `add_trusted_device(uuid, label)`, `revoke_device(uuid)`, `add_magic_link(token_hash, ...)`, `consume_magic_link(token)`, `purge_expired_magic_links()`. Hex-boundary forbidden imports: `web/`, `signal_engine`, `sizing_engine`, `notifier`, `dashboard`, `main`. Allowed: stdlib + `state_manager` (for shared atomic-write helper if extracted) — planner decides. Phase 18 (multi-user, v1.2) will migrate `auth.json` to SQLite.

- **F-02: Magic-link TTL = 1 hour (`max_age=3600` in `loads()`).** Token format: `URLSafeTimedSerializer(WEB_AUTH_SECRET, salt='magic-link').dumps({purpose, iat})`. The 1-hour expiry is enforced by `loads(token, max_age=3600)` AND independently by `auth.json.pending_magic_links[i].expires_at` field. Belt-and-suspenders against a bad clock. Token is `sha256`-hashed for storage so a leaked `auth.json` doesn't expose live tokens (the operator clicks the unhashed token in their email; server hashes-on-receive and compares).

- **F-03: Email transport = Resend, reusing existing `notifier.py` adapter.** From-sender = `signals@carbonbookkeeping.com.au` (existing verified Resend sender per PROJECT.md §Context). New helper in `notifier.py` (NOT in `web/`): `send_magic_link_email(to_email, link, action, expires_at)`. Idiomatically mirrors existing `send_signal_email` / `send_crash_email` shape. Subject: `"Trading Signals — 2FA reset link (valid 1 hour)"`. Body (HTML, single inline-CSS template — mirrors existing email templates per CLAUDE.md §Email rendering): plain language explanation, prominent button-link, expiry timestamp in operator's AWST timezone, fallback plain-text version. Logs: `[Email] magic link sent to=%s action=%s expires=%s`. Failures NEVER crash the request — log error and surface generic error to user (consistent with PROJECT.md "Error budget: App must never crash silently").

- **F-04: TOTP library = `pyotp`** (pinned exact version per CLAUDE.md §Stack). Defaults: 6 digits, SHA1, 30s period. `valid_window=1` in `verify()` allows ±30s clock drift. New requirements.txt entries: `pyotp==<latest_stable>`, `qrcode[pil]==<latest_stable>` (QR rendering for enrollment page), and confirm `itsdangerous` (D-10) is already pinned.

- **F-05: TOTP issuer label = `"Trading Signals"`.** Matches FastAPI app title (`web/app.py:88`). Account label format = `<username>@signals.mwiriadi.me` so the authenticator app shows `Trading Signals (marc@signals.mwiriadi.me)`.

- **F-06: Reset email recipient is HARDCODED to `mwiriadi@gmail.com`.** New env var `OPERATOR_RECOVERY_EMAIL` (default `mwiriadi@gmail.com`, validated at boot per Phase 13 D-16 fail-closed pattern — must match `^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$`). Single-operator tool; the email address is the operator's. v1.2 multi-user phase will move this per-user.

- **F-07: Login form gets a "Lost 2FA? Reset via email" link UNDER the submit button** (per E-07). Visually de-emphasized (small font, secondary colour). Click → expand inline form requiring username + password BOTH (matches login form fields exactly — same `<input>` shapes, same constant-time validation, same generic error). On submit success → generic confirmation page `"Check your email"`. Same response shape regardless of whether credentials matched.

- **F-08: Rate limit on `/forgot-2fa` and `/reset-totp` and `/login` (TOTP-verify step).** Implementation: in-memory token-bucket via `auth_store.py` helper (NOT a new dependency). Per-IP: 5 attempts per 15 min on `/login` POST, 3 attempts per hour on `/forgot-2fa`, 10 attempts per hour on `/reset-totp` GET (validates token signature and expiry). Per-account: 3 magic links per 24h (count `auth.json.pending_magic_links` where `created_at > now - 24h`). On limit exceeded: `429 Too Many Requests` for non-browser, redirect to `/login?error=too_many_attempts` for browser. Replit/droplet single-instance is fine; v1.2 multi-instance would need Redis (deferred).

- **F-09: Plan structure CHANGES (supersedes prior 2-plan layout).** With Basic Auth dropped (E-01) and TOTP added (E-03..E-08), the planner should regenerate plans as either 2 or 3 plans (planner's discretion per scope sanity):
  - **Option A (2 plans, sequential):** 16.1-01 = cookie login + TOTP enrollment + TOTP verify + auth_store.py + auth.json schema; 16.1-02 = trusted-device cookie + /devices page + magic-link reset flow + email helper + rate limits.
  - **Option B (3 plans, sequential):** 16.1-01 = auth_store.py + cookie login + TOTP enrollment/verify (core auth path); 16.1-02 = trusted-device cookie + /devices page; 16.1-03 = magic-link reset + email helper + rate limits.
  - Recommended: **Option B** — three smaller plans give cleaner test boundaries and let the operator UAT each layer independently (cookie+TOTP works → add trusted-device → add reset). Planner picks based on plan-size budget per CLAUDE.md.

### Claude's Discretion (Area E + F)

- **Username field on `/verify-totp` page** — typically not shown (already proven by valid `tsi_pending` cookie). Planner: omit username field on verify page; just show 6-digit input + trust-checkbox + submit.
- **`tsi_pending` and `tsi_enroll` cookie scope** — `Path=/`, `Max-Age=600` (10 min), `Secure; HttpOnly; SameSite=Strict`, signed with `WEB_AUTH_SECRET`. Mirror `tsi_session` attrs.
- **`/devices` page styling** — mirror dashboard.py inline-CSS palette per existing convention. Table layout. "(this device)" highlight via background-tint.
- **Last-seen update batching** — every authenticated request via `tsi_trusted` updates `last_seen`. Naive: write auth.json on every hit. Better: in-memory dirty flag, flush every 60s OR on shutdown (`atexit`). Planner picks based on web-process-lifecycle preferences. If naive write is <5ms with file-size in single-digit KB, naive is fine.
- **Magic-link email body styling** — planner mirrors existing `notifier.py` email-template aesthetic (dark theme per Phase 6, mobile-responsive inline CSS).
- **Recovery code generation** — NOT generated. User opted for email-only reset (E-07). `auth.json.recovery_codes` field omitted.
- **Sign Out button (D-13 reaffirmed) — needs a "Manage devices" sibling link.** Render only when cookie session detected. Two buttons/links: "Manage devices" → `/devices`, "Sign out" → `POST /logout`.
- **Migration path from auth.json to SQLite (Phase 18, v1.2)** — auth.json's schema_version field (F-01) lets Phase 18 detect and migrate. Out of scope for 16.1.
- **`pyotp.random_base32()` CSPRNG** — pyotp uses `secrets.token_bytes` under the hood. Adequate for TOTP secret entropy (160 bits > the 80-bit RFC 6238 floor).
- **Cookie ordering on response** — when issuing both `tsi_session` AND `tsi_trusted` (E-05), browsers handle multiple `Set-Cookie` headers natively. Planner emits them as separate response headers, not a single combined header.
- **Test file naming for new code** — `tests/test_auth_store.py` (auth_store.py — pure I/O, not in `web/`), `tests/test_web_auth_totp.py` (TOTP enrollment + verify routes), `tests/test_web_auth_devices.py` (/devices page + revocation), `tests/test_web_auth_reset.py` (magic-link reset flow), `tests/test_notifier_magic_link.py` (email helper). Mirror Phase 13/14 naming.

</decisions>

<canonical_refs>
## Canonical References

**Downstream agents (researcher, planner, executor) MUST read these before planning or implementing.**

### Phase spec + scope
- `.planning/ROADMAP.md` §Phase 16.1 — phase goal, success criteria, depends-on Phase 13 (loosened from "Phase 16" per insertion-commit note 2026-04-27)
- `.planning/REQUIREMENTS.md` — AUTH-04..AUTH-12 (Phase 16.1 scope expanded 2026-04-29: AUTH-08 TOTP enrollment, AUTH-09 TOTP verify, AUTH-10 trusted-device cookie + revocation, AUTH-11 magic-link reset, AUTH-12 Basic Auth removal)
- `.planning/PROJECT.md` §Out of Scope — "Multi-user accounts / auth — single-operator tool" hard constraint; Phase 16.1 only adds operator-UX paths, never multi-user
- `.planning/todos/pending/2026-04-27-phone-friendly-auth-ux-for-dashboard-access.md` — folded source todo with the problem narrative, 4 candidate evaluation, operator's lean, and constraints
- `SPEC.md` §v1.2+ Long-Term Roadmap (Reference) — Captured 2026-04-29. Phase 18 (multi-user data model) will migrate `auth.json` → SQLite per F-01.

### Prior-phase decisions that constrain Phase 16.1
- `.planning/phases/13-auth-read-endpoints/13-CONTEXT.md`:
  - D-01 — `AuthMiddleware` is the SOLE enforcement chokepoint via `app.add_middleware()`. Phase 16.1 extends this middleware in-place, never adds a parallel middleware or per-route `Depends`.
  - D-02 — `EXEMPT_PATHS = frozenset({'/healthz'})`. Phase 16.1 does NOT add new exempt paths; `/login` and `/logout` are auth-aware (the middleware handles them with awareness that they're the auth-bootstrap routes — no recursion).
  - D-03 — `hmac.compare_digest` for secret comparison; Phase 16.1 reuses for username AND secret comparison in Basic Auth and cookie-validation paths.
  - D-04 — original "no `WWW-Authenticate` header on 401, plain-text `unauthorized` body" — Phase 16.1 RECONCILES per D-04..D-05 above (browser-conditional 302 → /login; no `WWW-Authenticate` ever sent; curl/script 401 contract verbatim).
  - D-05 — audit log shape `[Web] auth failure: ip=%s ua=%r path=%s` from `X-Forwarded-For`. Phase 16.1 reuses unchanged for failed Basic Auth and failed cookie-validation attempts.
  - D-06 — `AuthMiddleware` registered LAST in `create_app()` so it runs FIRST (Starlette reverse-order). Phase 16.1 maintains this; new routes (`/login`, `/logout`) register BEFORE the middleware line per D-06 spirit.
  - D-16/D-17 — `WEB_AUTH_SECRET` fail-closed boot validation pattern. Phase 16.1 D-08 extends to `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` (different validation rules — non-empty + no `:`).
  - D-18 — Secret read ONCE at module load. Phase 16.1 mirrors for `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME`.
- `.planning/phases/14-trade-journal-mutation-endpoints/14-CONTEXT.md` (or its plan/summary):
  - Plan 14-04 Task 5 (REVIEWS HIGH #4) — `dashboard.html` placeholder substitution discipline. `web/routes/dashboard.py` substitutes `{{WEB_AUTH_SECRET}}` at request time so on-disk file never carries the real secret. Phase 16.1 D-13 keeps this UNCHANGED — zero diff to substitution code or tests.
  - HTMX `hx-headers` propagation pattern in `dashboard.py::_render_open_form` (line 1037..1039) and `dashboard.py::_render_position_table_section` (line 1562..1565) — Phase 16.1 leaves both untouched per D-13.

### Architectural invariants (do not break)
- `CLAUDE.md` §Architecture — hex-lite. `web/middleware/` is an adapter hex (Phase 13 established). Allowed imports for `web/middleware/auth.py`: `fastapi`, `starlette`, stdlib (`hmac`, `os`, `logging`, `base64`, `binascii`, `typing`), and `itsdangerous` (new dep per D-10). Forbidden imports: `signal_engine`, `sizing_engine`, `system_params`, `data_fetcher`, `notifier`, `dashboard`, `main`, `state_manager`. Enforced by `tests/test_web_healthz.py::TestWebHexBoundary`.
- `CLAUDE.md` §Conventions — 2-space indent, single quotes, snake_case, `[Web]` log prefix for ALL web-process log lines.
- `CLAUDE.md` §Stack — version pins are exact (`==`), no `>=`. `itsdangerous` (D-10) and any other new dep gets a specific pinned version in `requirements.txt`.
- Global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` §Cookie deletion must match cookie creation options — D-12 honors this verbatim (Set-Cookie deletion includes Path=/, Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict).
- Global `~/.claude/CLAUDE.md` §"Null tenantId = global access" is almost always wrong — N/A here (single-operator), but the spirit applies: we explicitly validate every request, never assume "no auth = welcome".

### Source files touched by Phase 16.1 (REVISED 2026-04-29 for TOTP fold-in)

**Updated touch list — supersedes prior list. Basic Auth files removed; TOTP/devices/reset files added.**

- `web/middleware/auth.py` — extend `AuthMiddleware.dispatch` per E-02 (3-step sniff: cookie → header → unauth-branch). NO Basic Auth decode (E-01). Add `_try_cookie`, `_try_header`, `_branch_unauthenticated` helpers.
- `web/app.py` — extend `_read_auth_secret` to `_read_auth_credentials` returning a `(username, secret, recovery_email)` tuple per D-08 + F-06. Thread username into `AuthMiddleware.__init__`. Register new routes (login, enroll, verify, devices, reset) BEFORE `add_middleware` line (Phase 13 D-06). New env-var validation: `OPERATOR_RECOVERY_EMAIL` (default `mwiriadi@gmail.com`, must match basic email regex).
- `web/routes/login.py` (new) — `GET /login` (form + "Lost 2FA?" link), `POST /login` (validates pwd → branches: enrollment vs verify-totp vs trusted-device), `POST /logout` (clears `tsi_session` and optionally `tsi_trusted` if user opts to "sign out everywhere"), `POST /forgot-2fa` (generic response, sends email).
- `web/routes/totp.py` (new) — `GET /enroll-totp` (renders QR + manual secret + verify field; gated by `tsi_enroll` cookie), `POST /enroll-totp` (validates 6-digit code → finalises enrollment → sets `tsi_session`), `GET /verify-totp` (renders 6-digit input + "trust this device" checkbox; gated by `tsi_pending` cookie), `POST /verify-totp` (validates code → sets `tsi_session` and optionally `tsi_trusted`).
- `web/routes/devices.py` (new) — `GET /devices` (auth-required, cookie-session-only, renders trusted-device table), `POST /devices/revoke` (flips revoked=True for one uuid), `POST /devices/revoke-all` (flips revoked=True for all uuids except current).
- `web/routes/reset.py` (new) — `GET /reset-totp?token=<...>` (validates magic-link token, marks consumed, sets one-time `tsi_session`, redirects to `/enroll-totp?reset=1`).
- `web/templates/` (new dir, OPTIONAL — only if Jinja2 chosen) — `login.html`, `enroll.html`, `verify.html`, `devices.html`. Planner discretion on inline-HTML-in-route-handlers vs Jinja templates; mirror `dashboard.py::_INLINE_CSS` palette either way.
- `auth_store.py` (new, repo root — peer of `state_manager.py`, NOT inside `web/`) — atomic JSON load/save for `auth.json` (F-01 schema). Helpers: `get_totp_secret`, `set_totp_secret`, `mark_enrolled`, `add_trusted_device`, `revoke_device`, `revoke_all_other_devices`, `get_trusted_device`, `update_last_seen`, `add_magic_link`, `consume_magic_link`, `purge_expired_magic_links`. Hex-boundary forbidden imports: `web/`, `signal_engine`, `sizing_engine`, `notifier`, `dashboard`, `main`. Allowed: stdlib, `state_manager` (atomic-write helper if extracted), `itsdangerous` (token signing/verifying).
- `notifier.py` — ADD `send_magic_link_email(to_email, link, action, expires_at)` per F-03. Reuses existing Resend transport pattern. Logs `[Email] magic link sent to=%s action=%s expires=%s`. Failures non-crashing (per existing convention).
- `system_params.py` — add TOTP-related constants if needed (issuer label, magic-link TTL, trust-device TTL, rate-limit thresholds — kept here for testability per existing convention). Pure data, no logic.
- `tests/test_auth_store.py` (new) — atomic write under crash, schema migration (v0→v1), trusted-device add/revoke, magic-link add/consume/expire/purge, concurrent-write tempfile collision behaviour.
- `tests/test_web_auth_middleware.py` (existing Phase 13) — extend with browser-vs-script Sec-Fetch sniff tests and 302-redirect tests, AND new tests for `tsi_trusted` short-circuit. Existing tests must keep passing unchanged (AUTH-05).
- `tests/test_web_routes_login.py` (new) — `GET /login` renders, `POST /login` valid creds → branches correctly (enrollment if new, verify-totp if enrolled, sets `tsi_session` if `tsi_trusted` valid), `POST /login` invalid creds → re-render + "Sign in failed", `POST /forgot-2fa` always returns generic page, rate limit kicks in at 3rd attempt per IP per hour.
- `tests/test_web_routes_totp.py` (new) — TOTP enrollment flow (gated by `tsi_enroll` cookie, QR rendered, valid code finalises, invalid code re-renders), verify flow (gated by `tsi_pending` cookie, valid code → `tsi_session`, "trust device" checkbox → `tsi_trusted` issued, invalid code → re-render).
- `tests/test_web_routes_devices.py` (new) — `/devices` page renders trusted-device list, "(this device)" highlight correct, `POST /devices/revoke` flips revoked, `POST /devices/revoke-all` flips all except current, header-path requests get 403.
- `tests/test_web_routes_reset.py` (new) — magic-link reset flow (valid token → consumed + redirect to enroll, expired token → 401, consumed token → 401, tampered token signature → 401).
- `tests/test_notifier_magic_link.py` (new) — magic-link email send (HTML body, plain-text fallback, subject format, log line shape, Resend failure non-crashing).
- `tests/test_web_app_factory.py` (existing Phase 13) — extend with `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` validation (D-08) AND `OPERATOR_RECOVERY_EMAIL` validation (F-06: missing → RuntimeError, malformed → RuntimeError, valid → boots).
- `requirements.txt` — pin `itsdangerous==<latest_stable>` (D-10), `pyotp==<latest_stable>` (F-04), `qrcode[pil]==<latest_stable>` (F-04). Exact-pin per CLAUDE.md §Stack.
- `SETUP-DROPLET.md` — extend "Configure auth secret" with: (a) `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` step, (b) `OPERATOR_RECOVERY_EMAIL` step (default `mwiriadi@gmail.com`), (c) first-login enrollment walkthrough (visit, enter creds, scan QR with Authenticator app, type 6-digit code), (d) recovery walkthrough (lost phone → click "Lost 2FA?" → check email → click link → choose "set up new authenticator"), (e) trusted-device management (`/devices` route).
- `.gitignore` — add `auth.json` line (mirror `state.json` entry).
- `dashboard.py` — ADD "Sign out" + "Manage devices" links/buttons (Claude's-Discretion in Area E + F). Render only when cookie session detected. DOES NOT modify existing `hx-headers` placeholder logic (D-13 unchanged).
- `tests/test_dashboard.py` (or new `tests/test_dashboard_auth_ui.py`) — assert sign-out + manage-devices links render only when cookie-session boolean is True.
- `.planning/phases/16.1-phone-friendly-auth-ux-for-dashboard-access/16.1-HUMAN-UAT.md` (new) — UAT scenarios:
  - UAT-16.1-A: iPhone Safari cold visit → /login → enter creds → enrollment QR → scan + verify → dashboard loads
  - UAT-16.1-B: Subsequent visit on same iPhone (no trust-device) → /login → enter creds → /verify-totp → enter 6-digit code → dashboard loads
  - UAT-16.1-C: Subsequent visit on same iPhone (with trust-device) → /login → enter creds → dashboard loads (no TOTP prompt)
  - UAT-16.1-D: Lost-phone scenario → click "Lost 2FA?" → enter creds → check Gmail → click link → choose "set up new authenticator" → scan + verify → dashboard loads
  - UAT-16.1-E: `/devices` → revoke a trusted device → revoked device's cookie no longer grants
  - UAT-16.1-F: curl with `X-Trading-Signals-Auth` header still returns 200 (AUTH-05 regression)
  - UAT-16.1-G: curl without header gets `401 unauthorized` plain-text (AUTH-07 contract preserved)

### Environment variables
- **NEW: `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME`** (required, non-empty, no `:`) — read by `create_app()` at import per D-08
- **NEW: `OPERATOR_RECOVERY_EMAIL`** (default `mwiriadi@gmail.com`, must match `^[^@]+@[^@]+\.[^@]+$`) — fail-closed boot validation per F-06
- Existing: `WEB_AUTH_SECRET` (required, ≥32 chars per Phase 13 D-17) — also doubles as the `itsdangerous` signing key per D-10 AND magic-link signing key per F-02
- Existing: `SIGNALS_EMAIL_FROM`, `SIGNALS_EMAIL_TO`, `RESEND_API_KEY` (signal loop — unchanged; magic-link reset email reuses these per F-03)

</canonical_refs>

<code_context>
## Existing Code Insights

### Reusable Assets
- `web/middleware/auth.py::AuthMiddleware` — Phase 13 ASGI middleware. Phase 16.1 extends in-place: adds Basic Auth decode (Plan 16.1-01) + cookie validation (Plan 16.1-02) BEFORE the existing header-validation branch per D-07 sniff order.
- `web/app.py::_read_auth_secret` — Phase 13 fail-closed env-var validation pattern. Phase 16.1 generalizes to `_read_auth_credentials` returning `(username, secret)` per D-08.
- `web/app.py::create_app` — Phase 13 factory. Phase 16.1 registers new `web/routes/login.py` BEFORE the `add_middleware` line per Phase 13 D-06 (Starlette reverse-registration order).
- `web/routes/healthz.register(app)` pattern — established Phase 11. `web/routes/login.py::register(app)` follows the same signature.
- `web/routes/dashboard.py::_PLACEHOLDER` substitution machinery — Phase 14 Plan 14-04 Task 5. Phase 16.1 leaves UNCHANGED (D-13 belt-and-suspenders).
- `dashboard.py::_render_open_form` and `dashboard.py::_render_position_table_section` HTMX `hx-headers` attribute — Phase 14 idiom. Phase 16.1 leaves UNCHANGED (D-13).
- `hmac.compare_digest` — already used Phase 13 D-03 for secret comparison; Phase 16.1 reuses for username comparison in Basic Auth path.
- `itsdangerous` — NEW dep per D-10. Provides `URLSafeTimedSerializer(secret_key, salt='web-auth-cookie').dumps({'u': username, 'iat': now})` and `loads(token, max_age=43200)`. ~5 lines of code in middleware.

### Established Patterns
- **Local imports inside handlers** — Phase 11 D-15 / Phase 13 C-2 convention. `web/routes/login.py` follows: `from state_manager import load_state` inside the handler, NOT module-top. Preserves hex boundary for `tests/test_web_healthz.py::TestWebHexBoundary::test_web_adapter_imports_are_local_not_module_top`.
- **`[Web]` log prefix** — Phase 11 convention. Phase 16.1 reuses: `[Web] login success: user=%s ip=%s`, `[Web] login failure: ip=%s ua=%r reason=%s` (reason is one of `wrong_username` / `wrong_secret` / `malformed_form` — log-only; client always sees the same generic "Sign in failed" message per D-14).
- **Test file naming `tests/test_web_<route>.py` and `tests/test_web_<feature>.py`** — Phase 11/13 conventions. Phase 16.1 follows: `tests/test_web_auth_basic.py`, `tests/test_web_auth_cookie.py`, `tests/test_web_routes_login.py`.
- **Factory pattern for testability** — `create_app()` reads env vars; tests `monkeypatch.setenv('WEB_AUTH_USERNAME', ...)` AND `monkeypatch.setenv('WEB_AUTH_SECRET', ...)` BEFORE calling `create_app()` in a fixture per Phase 13 D-18.
- **Atomic file writes** — N/A for cookies (cookies are HTTP-layer, not disk-state). N/A for `WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` (read-only env var, no disk persistence).
- **Open-redirect prevention** — D-14 success-redirect to `?next=<path>` MUST validate `next.startswith('/')` AND `'://' not in next` AND `':' not in next.split('/', 1)[0]` BEFORE issuing 302. Mirrors Django/Flask convention.

### Integration Points
- `AuthMiddleware.dispatch()` — single chokepoint extended per D-07 sniff order. The 4-step sequence is the integration point for ALL three new paths (Basic, cookie, header).
- `create_app()` — env-var validation (`WEB_AUTH_USERNAME` + `WEB_AUTH_SECRET`) happens BEFORE `FastAPI(...)` instantiation per Phase 13 D-16. systemd Restart=on-failure surfaces RuntimeError in journald.
- `web/routes/login.py::register(app)` — registers `GET /login`, `POST /login`, `POST /logout`. Routes are auth-aware: middleware lets them through if user is authenticated (cookie/Basic/header) AND if path matches; otherwise the login form-render endpoint is publicly accessible (otherwise login is impossible — chicken-and-egg). Same exemption mechanism as `EXEMPT_PATHS` per Phase 13 D-02 but with awareness: `EXEMPT_PATHS_PUBLIC = frozenset({'/healthz', '/login'})` for GET-method-only public access; POST /login is rate-limited but publicly accessible (auth bootstrap); POST /logout is auth-required (only authenticated users can log out).
- `dashboard.py::render_dashboard` — extended to accept an optional `is_cookie_session: bool = False` parameter. When True, renders a "Sign out" button (form POSTing to /logout). When False (header-only or Basic Auth via URL bar), no button rendered. Caller (`web/routes/dashboard.py::get_dashboard`) detects cookie session by inspecting `request.cookies.get('tsi_session')` AND validating it (reuses middleware helper).

</code_context>

<specifics>
## Specific Ideas

- **AUTH-04 verification scenario**: operator opens `https://signals.<owned-domain>.com/` on iPhone Safari with NO header-injection extension installed. Expected: 302 → /login → form renders → operator types `marc` (or whatever WEB_AUTH_USERNAME is set to) and the WEB_AUTH_SECRET → submit → 302 → / → dashboard renders with "Sign out" button visible. Counter-test: same flow on iPhone Chrome. Counter-test: `https://marc:<secret>@signals.<owned-domain>.com/` (URL-bar Basic Auth) → dashboard renders directly (Basic Auth path, no /login involvement).
- **AUTH-05 regression test**: `curl -H "X-Trading-Signals-Auth: <secret>" https://signals.<owned-domain>.com/` returns 200 with byte-identical dashboard HTML to a pre-16.1 baseline (or as close to byte-identical as possible — the Sign Out button addition per Claude's-Discretion may shift bytes; if so, the test asserts header-path still returns 200 + non-empty body, not byte-equality).
- **AUTH-06 enforcement test**: `grep -rn "==.*WEB_AUTH_SECRET\|WEB_AUTH_SECRET.*==" web/ tests/ | grep -v "compare_digest\|test_" | wc -l` returns 0. NO new `==` comparison against `WEB_AUTH_SECRET` is introduced; all comparisons go through `hmac.compare_digest`.
- **AUTH-07 contract preservation**: `curl https://signals.<owned-domain>.com/healthz` returns 200 with no auth (Phase 13 EXEMPT_PATHS unchanged). `curl https://signals.<owned-domain>.com/` returns `401 unauthorized` plain-text (no `WWW-Authenticate`, no `Set-Cookie`, no `Location`) — script-shaped request defaults to script-shaped 401. Browser-shaped request returns 302 → /login.
- **D-04 sniff implementation note**: `Sec-Fetch-Mode` was added in Chrome 76 (Aug 2019), Firefox 90 (July 2021), Safari 14.1 (Apr 2021). Effectively universal in 2026 for the operator's iPhone (Safari 17+). Fallback to `'text/html' in request.headers.get('accept', '').lower()` covers older clients.
- **`POST /login` rate limit**: defer to nginx layer follow-up (deferred ideas). Within FastAPI, no in-process rate limiter — Phase 13 D-Claude's-Discretion deferred this to nginx for `/` and `/api/state`; same precedent for `/login`.
- **Cookie scope**: `Path=/` (cookie sent on every request, including `/healthz` — but middleware's `EXEMPT_PATHS` short-circuits before cookie validation per Phase 13 D-02 + D-07 step 1). `Domain` attribute NOT set (cookie defaults to exact host `signals.mwiriadi.me` — operator only ever browses one host). No `Domain=.mwiriadi.me` because we don't want the cookie sent to other subdomains.
- **`Secure` attribute**: cookie ONLY sent over HTTPS. In test environment (TestClient over HTTP), use `Secure=True` anyway — test the production-shape cookie. Tests should use HTTPS-shaped client (or accept that `Secure` cookies are dropped in test client and assert presence in `Set-Cookie` header text directly).

</specifics>

<deferred>
## Deferred Ideas

- **Cloudflare Access (Zero Trust SSO)** — todo Option 3. Re-orange-cloud DNS + DNS-01 LE renewal + Cloudflare API token + certbot-dns-cloudflare. Architectural change. v1.2 candidate IF the cookie+Basic UX from this phase doesn't satisfy the operator OR if multi-user becomes desired (Cloudflare Access supports adding users to the policy without code changes).
- **Magic link via Resend** — todo Option 4. Requires the cookie-session machinery from this phase PLUS one-time-token generation + email send-on-login + token-consumption flow. v1.2 candidate IF the operator decides typing the password is too high friction (it isn't; Safari Keychain auto-fills).
- **Passkeys / WebAuthn** — modern iOS-native passwordless auth. Requires WebAuthn server library + ceremony state management + recovery flow. Significant complexity for single-operator tool. Park indefinitely; revisit if multi-user OR if the secret rotation problem becomes acute.
- **`POST /login` rate limiting at nginx layer** — Phase 13 D-Claude's-Discretion deferred rate-limit on `/` and `/api/state`. Same precedent here. Add an nginx zone `limit_req zone=login burst=5 nodelay` on `/login` if `[Web] login failure:` log noise becomes acute. Otherwise defer.
- **`Domain=.<root>` cookie attribute** — would let the cookie work across subdomains (e.g., if v1.2 adds `api.signals.<root>` or similar). Not needed today. Per global LEARNINGS "Cookie deletion must match cookie creation options" — if we ever add `Domain=`, the deletion code in D-12 MUST match.
- **Cookie session storage on server** — currently stateless (all session data signed into cookie payload). If session-revocation becomes needed (e.g., "log out all devices" button), need a server-side session store (Redis or even a JSON file on disk). Defer indefinitely; cookie-only is adequate for single-operator.
- **CSRF token machinery** — `SameSite=Strict` cookie + single-form-per-origin = no CSRF surface for single-operator tool. Skip.
- **Login attempt audit log distinct from auth-failure log** — Phase 13 D-05 audit log already covers auth failures generically. Currently `[Web] login failure: reason=wrong_username` reuses the existing log shape with an extra `reason=` field. If `[Web] login failure:` audit volume grows, split into separate `web-auth.log` channel via Python logging filters. Defer.
- **`/logout` GET method redirect to confirmation page** — currently POST-only (D-12). If operator wants a "sign out" link in the URL bar (typing `/logout` and pressing enter), add GET → render-with-confirm-button → POST. Defer; the form-button on the dashboard is sufficient.
- **Best-effort 401-eviction trick on /logout** — some browsers clear cached Basic Auth creds when they see a fresh 401 without matching `WWW-Authenticate`. Behavior inconsistent (Safari/Chrome differ; iOS especially flaky). Per D-12 we explicitly skip this and document the limitation honestly. Revisit IF operator complains about "I logged out but Safari keeps re-auto-filling".
- **Login form CSS extraction to shared stylesheet** — currently `dashboard.py::_INLINE_CSS` is the one source of truth. If `web/templates/login.html` (per Claude's-Discretion) duplicates significant CSS, extract to `web/static/styles.css` AND update Phase 13's `web/app.py` to mount static files. Defer; mirror dashboard.py palette inline first.
- **`/login` and `/logout` route exhaustion testing under high load** — single-operator means N=1 concurrent. Defer load testing.

### Reviewed Todos (not folded)
None — only one matching todo (`2026-04-27-phone-friendly-auth-ux-for-dashboard-access.md`) and it was folded per D-15.

</deferred>
